entities.name: GENOMIC SELECTION
entities.description: Methods used in plant breeding to select favorable lines using genomic data before performing field trials

entities.name: DIMENSIONALITY REDUCTION
entities.description: Pre-processing methods used to reduce the number of features in high-dimensional genomic marker data to improve efficiency

entities.name: GENOMIC MARKER DATA
entities.description: High-dimensional data yielded by genotyping technology used as input for statistical models in genomic prediction

entities.name: GENOTYPING TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: Technology used to generate high-dimensional genomic marker data

entities.name: STATISTICAL MODELS
entities.description: Models used to incorporate genomic marker data and evaluate effects of line, environment, and markers

entities.name: PREDICTION ACCURACY
entities.description: The measure of the correlation between predicted and observed values used to evaluate DR methods

entities.name: ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: The external conditions in which plant lines are phenotyped, contributing to genotype by environment interactions

entities.name: GENOTYPE BY ENVIRONMENT INTERACTION
entities.description: The interaction between the genetic makeup of a line and the environment it is grown in

entities.name: COMPUTATIONAL EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The reduction in computational resources and time required when using DR methods in GS models

entities.name: FIELD TRIALS
entities.description: The process of evaluating plant lines in the field to determine favorable traits

entities.name: LINES
entities.description: Plant breeding lines that are phenotyped and selected based on genomic data
